steps.title: Set up distance and time
steps.content: The total distance is $$r+b=200$$ Since time equals distance divided by speed, the biking time is $\frac{b}{10}$ and the running time is $\frac{r}{5}$.

steps.title: Use the time relationship
steps.content: Morgan biked for twice as many hours as she ran, so $$\frac{b}{10}=2\left(\frac{r}{5}\right)$$ Multiplying by $10$ gives $$b=4r$$

steps.title: Solve and confirm
steps.content: Substitute $b=4r$ into the distance equation: $$r+4r=200$$ $$r=40$$ Then $$b=4(40)=160$$ Choice D is correct.
